intake.premise_under_review: Parent witness 2026-04-27 mc-integrate-cli-conditional-resolution shipped modules/mc_integrate/cli.hexa as a thin dispatcher with 4-gate selftest PASS, but scoped-OBSTRUCTED the --compare path at runtime (exit 3, sentinel __MC_INTEGRATE_CLI__ OBSTRUCTED) because backend modules/mc_integrate/mc_integrate.hexa lacked (1) compare_rng() function and (2) Welch-t numerics (sample-size guard, t-stat threshold, NaN handling). Parent witness carry-forward (a) named this extension cycle.
intake.premise_corrected_after_audit: The OBSTRUCTION was correctly scoped. Forensic strings extraction from modules/mc_integrate/__pycache__/mc_integrate.cpython-314.pyc recovered the FULL compare_rng + Welch-t contract that was missing from README+cli.pyc strings: signature `compare_rng(integrand, dim, n_samples, n_trials=6) -> dict` with keys {anu_mean, anu_std, urandom_mean, urandom_std, t_stat, indistinguishable}; helper `_welch_t(a, b) -> (t_stat, indistinguishable_bool)` with verbatim docstring 'indistinguishable is True iff |t| < 2.0 (a coarse proxy for no-significant-difference at small n_trials)'; uses statistics.fmean / statistics.variance / statistics.pstdev. The hexa rewrite dispatches by named-constant (matching CLI `--compare <name>`) instead of arbitrary callable since hexa lacks first-class closure-as-arg ergonomics. Sample-size guard (n<2 -> undecided) and NaN handling (any NaN input -> undecided, NOT a fabricated p-value) added as honesty extensions; recovered code raises ZeroDivisionError on degenerate cases.

evidence_inputs.result: Full Welch-t signature recovered: '_welch_t(a,b) -> (t_stat, indistinguishable_bool); indistinguishable iff |t| < 2.0; uses statistics.fmean + variance/pstdev; compare_rng(integrand, dim, n_samples, n_trials=6) -> dict with keys anu_mean/anu_std/urandom_mean/urandom_std/t_stat/indistinguishable.'
Confirms three-function public API: estimate / estimate_constant / compare_rng. Confirms LCG params, gate epsilons, ANU bootstrap path.

evidence_inputs.fact: Backend already exposes estimate_constant(name, n_samples, rng) -> map with the result-shape needed for compare_rng to compose: extracts r['value'] per trial.
Parent dispatcher already declares --compare/--trials surface in argparse + help; only _cmd_compare body needed swapping from OBSTRUCT to backend-route.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: compare_rng-signature-matches-recovered-cpython-pyc-strings
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: the implemented compare_rng signature + result-map keys must match the strings(mc_integrate.cpython-314.pyc) recovery: (anu_mean, anu_std, urandom_mean, urandom_std, t_stat, indistinguishable). Any divergence is fabrication.
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Implemented `fn compare_rng(name: string, n_samples: int, n_trials: int, rng_a: string, rng_b: string) -> map`. Result map contains all six recovered keys verbatim (anu_mean, anu_std, urandom_mean, urandom_std, t_stat, indistinguishable). Extension keys (df, undecided, reason, n_trials, samples, name, rng_a_used, rng_b_used, rng_a_requested, rng_b_requested) are documented in @origin block as additions, NOT pretended-recovered. Hexa surface dispatches by named-constant rather than arbitrary callable per recorded ergonomics gap; documented in @origin block.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — recovered keys verbatim; extensions explicitly marked

falsifiers_preregistered.name: welch-t-numerically-correct-on-identity-rng
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: two arrays of identical (or near-identical) values must yield t_stat ~= 0 and indistinguishable=true
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Self-test gates F2/F2b/F2c: identity arrays [1,1,1,1,1,1] vs [1,1,1,1,1,1] -> t_stat=0.0, df handled, indistinguishable=true. Near-identity [1.000,1.001,0.999,1.000,1.002,0.998] vs [1.001,0.999,1.000,1.001,0.998,1.002] -> t_stat=-0.20 |t|<2 indistinguishable=true df=9.98. Live compare_rng on catalan N=1000 trials=3 urandom-vs-urandom -> t_stat≈0.29-1.0 |t|<2 indistinguishable=true.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — F2 t=0.0; F2b t=-0.20; F2c live |t|<2

falsifiers_preregistered.name: welch-t-detects-biased-rng
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: two arrays with large mean separation (e.g. ~1.0 vs ~2.0) and low variance must yield t_stat >> 2.0 and indistinguishable=false
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Self-test gate F3: bias_a=[1.000,1.001,0.999,1.000,1.002,0.998] vs bias_b=[2.000,2.001,1.999,2.000,2.002,1.998] -> t_stat=-1224.74, |t|>>2.0, indistinguishable=false. Welch–Satterthwaite df=10.0 (matches expected n_a+n_b-2 for equal variances). Sentinel: __MC_INTEGRATE_COMPARE__ PASS t_stat=-1224.74 df=10 verdict=significant.

falsifiers_preregistered.name: sample-size-guard-prevents-tiny-n-false-positive
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: single-element arrays (n=1) must trigger undecided=true with reason=sample_size_too_small, NOT a fabricated p-value
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Self-test gate F4: tiny_a=[1.0], tiny_b=[2.0] -> undecided=true, reason='sample_size_too_small', t_stat=0.0 (placeholder, not a calculated value). Backend `compare_rng` also gates n_trials<2 -> undecided='n_trials_too_small'. CLI dispatcher exits 2 (not 0) on undecided to signal the honest deferral.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — tiny-n yields explicit undecided, not fake p-value

falsifiers_preregistered.name: NaN-handled-not-fabricated
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: NaN in any input array must trigger undecided=true with reason=nan_in_input, not propagate as a quiet NaN t_stat
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Self-test gate F5: nan_a=[1.0,2.0,nan,4.0] vs nan_b=[1.0,2.0,3.0,4.0] -> undecided=true, reason='nan_in_input'. _is_nan helper uses x!=x identity detection (NaN is the only float where this holds). Degenerate zero-variance with mean diff>0 also caught explicitly with reason='zero_variance_unequal_means' as undecided rather than div-by-zero.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — NaN explicitly intercepted; undecided returned with reason

falsifiers_preregistered.name: cli.hexa-compare-path-now-PASS-not-OBSTRUCT
falsifiers_preregistered.predicate: after extension, `hexa run modules/mc_integrate/cli.hexa --compare <name>` must exit 0 (or 2 on undecided) and emit __MC_INTEGRATE_CLI__ PASS sub=compare; OBSTRUCTED sentinel must be removed.
falsifiers_preregistered.evaluation: Live smoke `hexa run modules/mc_integrate/cli.hexa --compare zeta3 --N 2000 --trials 4 --rng-a urandom --rng-b urandom` -> exit 0 with `__MC_INTEGRATE_COMPARE__ PASS t_stat=-0.999 df=5.93 verdict=not-significant` and `__MC_INTEGRATE_CLI__ PASS sub=compare name=zeta3 N=2000 trials=4 rng_a=urandom rng_b=urandom verdict=not-significant`. JSON envelope variant emits {decision:'not-significant', verdict:'PASS'}. Self-test compare-live gate exit=0 OK; compare-unknown gate exit=1 OK. No OBSTRUCTED sentinel emitted on the --compare path.
falsifiers_preregistered.verdict: PASS — cli.hexa --compare path live; OBSTRUCTED removed

tier_gate.verdict: PASS — all 6 falsifiers PASS; tier-1 gate clears on both numerical-honesty and edge-case-honesty and cli-unblocked subgates

decision.verdict: PASS — backend extended with compare_rng + Welch-t; cli.hexa --compare path live

files_touched.action: EXTENDED — added _is_nan, _array_has_nan, _mean_arr, _sample_var, _pop_stdev, _welch_t (with sample-size + NaN + zero-variance guards), _is_known_constant_name, compare_rng, _welch_t_arrays (test entry), _print_compare. Self-test extended with F2/F2b/F3/F4/F5 + F2c live compare gates. main() extended with --compare/--trials/--rng-a/--rng-b flags and dispatch. Sentinels: __MC_INTEGRATE_COMPARE__ PASS|FAIL t_stat=<v> df=<v> verdict=<significant|not-significant|undecided>. @api annotation added; @origin extension block names this witness.

UPDATED — _cmd_compare body swapped from runtime OBSTRUCT (exit 3) to backend route via `hexa run mc_integrate.hexa --compare ...`; signature extended with n_samples, n_trials, rng_a, rng_b, emit_json, seed_hex; --json envelope parses verdict + decision from sentinel; self-test gate 2 swapped from compare-obstruction (exit 3) to compare-live (exit 0|2) plus new compare-unknown (exit 1) gate; main() parses --trials/--rng-a/--rng-b; @tool desc + @origin updated to reflect live status; help text updated.

Recovered code uses flat 2.0 verbatim (docstring: 'a coarse proxy for no-significant-difference at small n_trials') so this is an enhancement not a correctness fix.
(d-compare/welch-p) optionally compute Welch p-value via series approximation of the regularized incomplete beta function; recovered code does NOT compute p-value, only the |t|<2 boolean.

report.headline: OBSTRUCTION resolved. Backend mc_integrate.hexa extended with compare_rng(name, n_samples, n_trials, rng_a, rng_b) -> map and Welch-t numerics (_welch_t with sample-size + NaN + zero-variance guards, |t|<2.0 threshold verbatim from recovered strings(mc_integrate.cpython-314.pyc), Welch–Satterthwaite df). cli.hexa --compare path now routes to backend; OBSTRUCTED sentinel removed. All 6 falsifiers PASS: F1 signature matches verbatim recovered keys; F2 identity arrays t=0.0; F2b near-identity t=-0.20 |t|<2; F3 biased arrays t=-1224.74 |t|>>2; F4 sample-size guard returns undecided=true reason=sample_size_too_small; F5 NaN handled with reason=nan_in_input; F6 cli.hexa --compare zeta3 --N 2000 --trials 4 exits 0 with verdict=not-significant.
report.next_action: Auto-ingest via tool/omega_cycle_atlas_ingest.hexa; outcome record at state/mc_integrate_compare_rng_backend_extension_2026-04-27.md.
